danger_signals.visual_form: Pursuing army visible on-screen or off-screen tracking distance meter
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; maintaining distance from enemy force is a time-limit mechanic

danger_signals.visual_form: Uniformed enemy soldiers, likely with distinctive visual markers
danger_signals.meaning: Organized terrorist opposition; heightened danger from coordinated military-style threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Captive character present in danger zones or being recaptured
danger_signals.meaning: Mission failure risk; rescuers must protect objective or restart stage

danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary turrets, fortified positions, armed guards clustered
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental combat hazards requiring strategic approach

safety_signals.visual_form: Rescue plane or helicopter visible at designated location
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; successful mission completion if hostage and heroes reach this point

safety_signals.visual_form: Rescued character moving with player characters (NES version: Elise follows Paul and Vince)
safety_signals.meaning: Primary objective achieved; transition to extraction phase

safety_signals.visual_form: Distance counter showing safe gap between pursuing army and player position
safety_signals.meaning: Temporal safety; game continues if distance > 0km

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crime Ghost branding/insignia (visual form not detailed in sources)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dangerous criminals, disgruntled soldiers, uniformed troops, commander-class units (Faust as supreme leader in NES version)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crime Ghost hideout facilities, fortified terrorist bases, command centers where hostages are held
Presidential residence, extraction points, safe government zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presidential setting, briefing/kidnapping location, blackmail letter delivery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Static exposition; president's desperation drives hiring of warriors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: On-screen distance meter tracking enemy army proximity; pursuing troops visible or implied through UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must advance continuously; stopping or moving backward increases distance danger; failure occurs at 0km separation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crime Ghost commanders and lieutenants present in fortified positions (e.g., stage 3 captors, Crime Ghost soldiers, final boss Faust in NES)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating resistance; main army realizes deception and returns with reinforcements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescue helicopter shot down; captive removed from player proximity; Crime Ghost forces consolidate position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission resets or extends; additional stages required to re-secure hostage before extraction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescue plane at designated extraction point; Crime Ghost soldiers making final stand; multiple hostiles concentrated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest intensity combat; all remaining Crime Ghost forces attempt to prevent escape; final boss encounter (NES version only: Faust)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Warriors boarding rescue plane with secured hostage; departure sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game completion; narrative closure revealing heroes' deed remains classified

environmental_storytelling.example: On-screen meter or HUD element showing 'km' gap between pursuing Crime Ghost army and player position; zero distance triggers stage failure/restart
Rescued character physically present with player party during extraction phase; visual confirmation of objective completion or failure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon scarcity and combat environment design
environmental_storytelling.example: Reduced weapon availability (vs. earlier Ikari games) signals higher-difficulty covert operation; hand-to-hand dominance reflects Rambo III inspiration of unarmed combat mastery
Sequential stages representing deeper infiltration into Crime Ghost territory; stage 1 outer perimeter → stage 3 command center → extraction zone at furthest depth
Crime Ghost commanders and troops visually distinguish themselves from generic soldiers; supreme leader Faust (NES) presented as final obstacle with heightened visual prominence
Fourth scuba-diving stage provides environmental story beat of expanded rescue complexity and underwater infiltration mechanic

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active combat engagement; guard positions at fortifications; patrol routes
Continuous advancement toward player position; return acceleration after deception revealed; distance-based pressure mechanic
Stationary when held by Crime Ghost; follows player after rescue; vulnerable during escort phase; mission failure if recaptured or killed

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Ralf Jones / Clark Still (Arcade); Paul / Vince (NES)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Offensive hand-to-hand and limited ranged combat; movement tied to hostage escort priority in final phase; stage restart on capture
Elite combat capability; positioned at extraction point as ultimate obstacle; mega-boss encounter design with heightened visual distinction

notes: Ikari III manifests political tension through a covert military narrative: kidnapped hostage of political figure → blackmail ultimatum → secret warrior deployment to avoid public knowledge. Visual communication relies heavily on pursuit mechanics (distance meter as core UI storytelling) and hostage state changes. Crime Ghost's global infiltration post-economic recession provides geopolitical context. The game uses environmental progression (outer perimeter → command center → extraction) to scaffold narrative advancement. NES version expands story with named characters (Paul, Vince, Elise, Faust) and additional stage, strengthening faction identity and political narrative. Hand-to-hand combat emphasis (vs. vehicle/weapon focus in earlier Ikari games) signals asymmetric covert operation rather than direct military engagement.
